briefdescription.para: Compute the CRC32 of the passed in buffer.

detaileddescription.para: Small lookup-table half-byte CRC32 algorithm. See https://create.stephan-brumme.com/crc32/#half-byte

parameterdescription.para: The buffer.
The length of the buffer.

simplesect.para: The CRC32 of the buffer.

briefdescription.para: Append a "crc" field to the passed in JSON buffer.

detaileddescription.para: The "crc" field has a value of the form "SSSS:CCCCCCCC", where SSSS is the passed in sequence number and CCCCCCCC is the CRC32.

parameterdescription.para: The JSON buffer to both add the CRC32 to and to compute the CRC32 over.
A 16-bit sequence number to include as a part of the CRC.

simplesect.para: A dynamically-allocated version of the passed in buffer with the CRC field added or NULL on error.

briefdescription.para: Check the passed in JSON for CRC and sequence number errors.

detaileddescription.para: If the calculated CRC32 doesn't match what's in the buffer, that's an error. If the sequence number in the buffer doesn't match shouldBeSeqno, that's an error. If there is no CRC field in the buffer, that's not an error UNLESS this function has been given a buffer with a CRC field before. In this case, the lack of a CRC field is an error.

parameterdescription.para: The JSON buffer for which the CRC should be checked. Note that the CRC is stripped from the input JSON regardless of whether or not there was an error.
The expected sequence number.

simplesect.para: true if there's an error and false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Suppress showing transaction details.
Resume showing transaction details.
Suppress showing transaction details.
Resume showing transaction details.
Create a new JSON request.

detaileddescription.para: Creates a dynamically allocated J object with one field "req" whose value is the passed in request string.

parameterdescription.para: The name of the request, for example hub.set.

simplesect.para: A J object with the "req" field populated.

briefdescription.para: Create a new JSON command.

detaileddescription.para: Create a dynamically allocated J object with one field "cmd" whose value is the passed in request string. The difference between a command and a request is that the Notecard does not send a response to commands, only to requests.

parameterdescription.para: The name of the command (e.g. card.attn).

simplesect.para: A J object with the "cmd" field populated.

briefdescription.para: Send a request to the Notecard.

detaileddescription.para: The passed in request object is always freed, regardless of if the request was successful or not. The response from the Notecard, if any, is freed and not returned to the caller.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to a J request object.

simplesect.para: true if successful and false if an error occurs (e.g. out of memory or the response from the Notecard has an "err" field). If req is a command rather than a request, a true return value indicates that the command was sent without error. However, since the Notecard sends no response to commands, it does not guarantee that the command was received and processed by the Notecard.
NoteRequestResponse if you need to work with the response.

briefdescription.para: Send a request to the Notecard, retrying it until it succeeds or it times out.

detaileddescription.para: The passed in request object is always freed, regardless of if the request was successful or not. The response from the Notecard, if any, is freed and not returned to the caller.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to a J request object.
Time limit for retires, in seconds, if there is no response, or if the response contains an I/O error.

simplesect.para: true if successful and false if an error occurs (e.g. out of memory or the response from the Notecard has an "err" field).
NoteRequestResponseWithRetry if you need to work with the response.

briefdescription.para: Send a request to the Notecard and return the response.

detaileddescription.para: The passed in request object is always freed, regardless of if the request was successful or not.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to a J request object.

simplesect.para: A J object with the response or NULL if there was an error sending the request.
NoteResponseError to check the response for errors.

briefdescription.para: Send a request to the Notecard, retrying it until it succeeds or it times out, and return the response.

detaileddescription.para: The passed in request object is always freed, regardless of if the request was successful or not.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to a J request object.
Time limit for retires, in seconds, if there is no response, or if the response contains an I/O error.

simplesect.para: A J object with the response or NULL if there was an error sending the request.
NoteResponseError to check the response for errors.

briefdescription.para: Send a request to the Notecard and return the response.

detaileddescription.para: Unlike NoteRequestResponse, this function expects the request to be a valid JSON C-string, rather than a J object. The string is expected to be newline-terminated, otherwise the call produces undefined behavior. The response is returned as a dynamically allocated JSON C-string. The response string is verbatim what was sent by the Notecard, which IS newline-terminated. The caller is responsible for freeing the response string. If the request was a command (i.e. it uses "cmd" instead of "req"), this function returns NULL, because the Notecard does not send a response to commands.

parameterdescription.para: A valid newline-terminated JSON C-string containing the request.

simplesect.para: A newline-terminated JSON C-string with the response, or NULL if there was no response or if there was an error.
When a "cmd" is sent, it is not possible to determine if an error occurred.

briefdescription.para: Send a request to the Notecard and return the response.

detaileddescription.para: This function doesn't free the passed in request object. The caller is responsible for freeing it.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to a J request object.

simplesect.para: A J object with the response or NULL if there was an error sending the request.
NoteResponseError to check the response for errors.

briefdescription.para: Same as NoteTransaction, but takes an additional parameter that indicates if the Notecard should be locked.

parameterdescription.para: The J cJSON request object.
Set to true if the Notecard should be locked and false otherwise.

simplesect.para: a J cJSON object with the response, or NULL if there is insufficient memory.

briefdescription.para: Mark that a reset will be required before doing further I/O on a given port.
Initialize or re-initialize the module.

simplesect.para: True if the reset was successful and false if not.

briefdescription.para: Check to see if a Notecard error is present in a JSON string.

detaileddescription.para: Only Notecard errors enclosed in {} (e.g. {io} for an I/O error) are guaranteed by the API.

parameterdescription.para: The string to check for errors.
The error substring to search for in errstr.

simplesect.para: true if errstr contains errtype and false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Clean error strings out of the specified buffer.

detaileddescription.para: Notecard errors are enclosed in {} (e.g. {io} for an I/O error). This function takes the input string and removes all errors from it, meaning it removes any substrings matching the pattern {some error string}, including the braces.

parameterdescription.para: A C-string to to clean of errors.

briefdescription.para: Convert a hex string to a 64-bit unsigned integer.

parameterdescription.para: The hex string to convert.
The length of the hex string.

simplesect.para: The converted number.

detaileddescription.para: Written by Ray Ozzie and Blues Inc. team.
Copyright (c) 2019 Blues Inc. MIT License. Use of this source code is governed by licenses granted by the copyright holder including that found in the LICENSE file.
